rationale.R1.2: 'What do you think?' on a technical artifact plausibly demands checking whether the two-stage pipeline's logic is sound, whether the ablation conclusions follow from Table 2, and whether the inference-time framing is internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The abstract makes three quantitative claims (COMET-22 deltas, NLLB gap, 62% human preference) and two priority claims ('first to…'); verifying that stated evidence actually supports those claims is load-bearing under a scientific-review reading.
rationale.R1.4: Two explicit 'first to our knowledge' claims make novelty a natural focal point of any opinion on this work; assessing the delta vs. prior context-injection and sense-disambiguation approaches in NMT is centrally relevant.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for a scientific/technical critique (methodology soundness, evidence-claim alignment, novelty verification) or for feedback on writing quality and presentation clarity?
